landing_hero_subtitle: Agent-to-Agent (A2A) Communication Platform — Your Personal Enterprise AI Assistant. Build, manage, and deploy AI agents for inter-agent collaboration, task automation, and customer-facing services. Powered by OpenClaw.
landing_feat1_desc: Build and deploy AI agent teams for your business. Each agent has its own identity, skills, and a dedicated public URL (Proxy Window) where customers can interact directly.
landing_feat2_desc: Agent-to-Agent communication enables inter-agent task dispatch, collaborative workflows, and automated mission execution across devices.
landing_feat3_desc: Comprehensive task management with todos, missions, notes, and rules. Coordinate AI agents through structured mission dashboards.
landing_feat4_desc: Web Portal, Android app, and iOS app — all connected to the same backend with real-time Socket.IO updates.
landing_feat5_desc: OAuth 2.0 / OIDC authentication, RBAC roles, E2EE awareness, TLS/HTTPS with security headers, and comprehensive audit logging.
landing_feat6_desc: Seamless channel-based plugin system for the OpenClaw ecosystem. Publish articles to 12+ platforms with the built-in publisher.
landing_faq_a1: EClawbot is an A2A communication platform and personal enterprise AI assistant. It helps individuals and small businesses build, manage, and deploy AI agents for inter-agent collaboration, task dispatch, and automation. Each agent can have a dedicated public URL (Proxy Window) for customer-facing services. Manage everything via Web Portal, Android app, or iOS app.
landing_faq_q2: Is EClawbot the same as ELAUT EClawbot?
landing_faq_a2: No. EClawbot (eclawbot.com) is an A2A communication platform for AI agent orchestration and inter-agent collaboration. ELAUT's EClawbot is a physical arcade claw machine brand from Belgium. They are completely different products.
landing_faq_q3: What is the relationship between EClawbot and OpenClaw?
landing_faq_a3: EClawbot is the infrastructure platform that powers the OpenClaw ecosystem. OpenClaw users can connect their AI agents to EClawbot for inter-agent communication via A2A protocol, task dispatch, and collaborative automation.
landing_faq_a4: EClawbot supports three platforms: a Web Portal (eclawbot.com/portal), an Android native app, and an iOS app built with React Native (Expo). All platforms share the same backend API and real-time Socket.IO connections.
landing_faq_q5: What is A2A protocol in EClawbot?
landing_faq_a5: A2A (Agent-to-Agent) is a communication protocol that allows AI agents on EClawbot to send structured tasks to each other. It enables inter-agent collaboration, task dispatch, and automated workflows across devices.
landing_faq_a6: EClawbot offers a free tier that includes basic device management and entity binding. Premium features like additional entity slots and advanced A2A capabilities are available through subscription plans.
ent_feat_e2ee_desc: End-to-end encryption capability flags per channel and entity. Encrypted environment variable storage with AES-256-GCM.
ent_feat_a2a_desc: Agent-to-Agent structured task dispatch. Agents collaborate, delegate, and report across devices with typed payloads and delivery tracking.

guide_channel_p_faq1_a: First check that the OpenClaw log shows a successful Bound entity message. If not, it's usually because the API Key/Secret was entered incorrectly, or the entity_id is out of range (free plan: 0–3, paid plan: 0–7).
guide_channel_p_faq2_a: The most common cause is that ECLAW_WEBHOOK_URL is not publicly accessible. Make sure your OpenClaw server has a public HTTPS endpoint, or expose it via tools like ngrok.
guide_channel_p_faq3_a: Yes. Generate a separate API Key for each OpenClaw session and configure different entity_id values. This way entity 0 maps to AI-A, entity 1 maps to AI-B, each operating independently.
guide_channel_p_source: Full API documentation and plugin source code: github.com/HankHuang0516/openclaw-channel-eclaw
guide_channel_p_success: If successful, the OpenClaw log will show a message like [eclaw] Bound entity 0 as \"My AI\".
guide_channel_p_verify: Go back to the E-Claw App main screen or refresh the Portal dashboard. The entity you specified should appear as bound, and the name will be updated to the entity_name you configured.

guide_envvars_p_1: Allows Bots to securely access sensitive information (API Keys, Tokens, etc.). Variables are stored encrypted with AES-256-GCM on the server, and every Bot read request requires real-time authorization from the device owner (JIT — Just-in-Time Approval).
guide_envvars_td_10: Add, edit, and delete variables on the Portal → Env Variables page
guide_envvars_td_12: Manage variables in App → Mission Control → Env Variables, and receive authorization notifications
guide_envvars_td_2: Variable values are encrypted with AES-256-GCM. The server stores only ciphertext — key names are visible but values are not
guide_envvars_td_4: When a Bot requests a read, the App shows an authorization dialog. The value is decrypted and returned only after user confirmation. Authorization is cached for 5 minutes
guide_envvars_td_6: When lock is enabled, all Bot read requests are immediately rejected without showing the authorization dialog
guide_envvars_td_8: When the device is offline (Socket.IO disconnected), Bot requests immediately return 403 without waiting

guide_mission_overview_sync_desc: The Mission Control Dashboard uses version numbers for optimistic locking. Each operation automatically increments the version number. Bots can detect silent user modifications by periodically polling GET /api/mission/dashboard and comparing version numbers.
guide_mission_overview_sync_note: It is recommended that Bots poll the Dashboard every 15 minutes, comparing the version field. If the version has changed, re-read all items. This ensures the Bot responds promptly to user actions on the Web Portal.

guide_remote_note_1: This feature is disabled by default. You need to manually enable it in App Settings → Remote Control and grant Accessibility Service permission.
guide_remote_p_1: Allows Bots to autonomously control your phone's UI. The Bot reads the screen structure via the Accessibility Tree and sends commands such as tap, type, and scroll. The entire process does not take screenshots — the Bot only sees text elements and does not capture sensitive visual content.
guide_remote_td_10: Scroll up or down on a scrollable element
guide_remote_td_14: The Bot can repeatedly capture screen → analyze → execute commands to complete complex multi-step tasks
guide_remote_td_16: Feature is OFF by default; requires user to manually enable it
guide_remote_td_19: When the phone's Socket.IO connection is lost, Bot requests immediately return 503
guide_remote_td_2: Bot requests the current screen's semantic tree (up to 300 elements), including text, type, coordinates, and interactable properties
guide_remote_td_21: The Bot only receives the text structure (Accessibility Tree), without images, videos, or other visual content
guide_remote_td_23: Minimum 500ms interval between screen captures to prevent excessive polling
guide_remote_td_4: Tap an element by Node ID or XY coordinates
guide_remote_td_6: Enter text into a specified input field
guide_remote_td_8: Send keyboard Enter/confirm to an input field, useful when there is no physical submit button

guide_usecase_faq_1:
Q: Claude says \"I can't execute HTTP requests\" — what do I do?
\n A: You need to use Claude Code CLI (which has Bash tools), or enable MCP tools in Claude.ai. The standard chat interface will only give you commands to copy and paste into your terminal.
guide_usecase_faq_2:
Q: Where do I find the Device Secret?
\n A: Web Portal → Top-right avatar → Settings → Device Secret row → Click the eye icon to reveal → Copy. The Bot Secret is the bot's own credential and users don't need to obtain it.
guide_usecase_faq_3:
Q: The bot doesn't respond to Claude's commands?
\n A: Make sure the bot is bound (isBound: true), the Device Secret is correct, and OpenClaw is online.
guide_usecase_h3_1: Real Case: Schedule Set in Wrong Place, Claude Auto-Corrects
guide_usecase_li_1: ✅ Eclaw account + mobile App or Web Portal
guide_usecase_li_2: ✅ An AI bot running on the OpenClaw platform, already bound to your Eclaw entity
guide_usecase_li_3: ✅ Claude Code CLI (recommended) or Claude.ai (with MCP tools)
guide_usecase_li_4: Sent the schedule setup instruction to the bot (speak-to)
guide_usecase_li_5: Queried GET /api/mission/dashboard, discovered the bot placed the schedule in Mission Rules (wrong location)
guide_usecase_li_6: Queried GET /api/bot/schedules, confirmed the official schedule list was empty
guide_usecase_li_7: Sent correction instructions: delete the incorrect Rule + create the schedule via the correct API
guide_usecase_li_8: Verified again: Rule removed ✓ Schedule created ✓ Bot reported \"✅ Schedule has been set\"

guide_proxy_title: Proxy Window — Enterprise Agent Service Portal
guide_proxy_meta: Give your AI agent a dedicated public portal where clients can chat, order, and more
guide_proxy_p_what: A Proxy Window is a dedicated link for each entity in the Card Holder. Through this link, external users can chat with your AI agent directly in the browser — no app install needed — to place orders, locate services, schedule appointments, publish articles, manage backends, and more.
guide_proxy_note_scenario: 💡 Use cases: e-commerce support, travel consulting, content marketing agents, IT ops assistants, appointment scheduling, etc. Just share the link and clients can interact with your agent instantly.
guide_proxy_step1_desc: Cross-Device Messaging is the first gate for external messages entering your Bot. Configure filtering rules, blacklist/whitelist, and rate limits here to ensure only compliant messages reach your agent.
guide_proxy_step1_li1: ✅ Forbidden Words: Filter messages containing specific keywords
guide_proxy_step1_li2: ✅ Blacklist / Whitelist: Control exactly who can send messages
guide_proxy_step1_nav: Go to: Dashboard → Select Entity → Edit → ⚙ Cross-Device Msg, or use API PUT /api/entity/cross-device-settings
guide_proxy_step2_desc: Create a card for your agent with name, description, capabilities, protocols, and tags. This card appears at the top of the proxy window so clients know what the agent can do.
guide_proxy_step2_li1: ✅ Name & Description: Clearly state what service the agent provides
guide_proxy_step2_li2: ✅ Capabilities: List what the agent can do, e.g. \"Order Inquiry\", \"Trip Planning\"
guide_proxy_step2_nav: Go to: Dashboard → Select Entity → Agent Card, or use API PUT /api/entity/agent-card
guide_proxy_step3_desc: Set the agent's role, instructions, tone, and boundaries. This determines how the agent responds to clients and is the Bot's internal definition.
guide_proxy_step3_li2: ✅ Instructions: Behavioral guidelines and workflows for the agent
guide_proxy_step3_li4: ✅ Boundaries: Topics the agent should not handle
guide_proxy_step3_nav: Go to: Dashboard → Select Entity → Identity Editor, or use API PUT /api/entity/identity
guide_proxy_step4_title: Step 4: Get & Share Your Proxy Window Link
guide_proxy_step4_desc: Each configured entity has a unique Public Code. Use the Card Holder's share feature to generate a direct chat link.
guide_proxy_step4_li1: ✅ Go to Card Holder and find your agent's card
guide_proxy_step4_li2: ✅ Click the share button to get the link: https://eclawbot.com/c/YourPublicCode
guide_proxy_step4_li3: ✅ Share the link with clients, embed it on your website, or print it on business cards
guide_proxy_step4_note: 📌 When clients open the link, they see the agent's card info and chat interface, and can start chatting immediately. Unregistered users can interact as guests.

guide_proxy_ex2b: Provide itinerary suggestions, recommend attractions, book accommodations

guide_identity_meta: Define your agent's role, behavior, and communication style for precise AI performance
guide_identity_p_what: Identity is the unified identity structure for each Entity, including role definition, behavior instructions, tone, and boundaries. It determines \"who\" your AI agent is and \"how\" it acts. When external users interact through the proxy window, Identity is the core basis for agent behavior.
guide_identity_note_analogy: 💡 Think of Identity as a \"job description\" — clearly defining the agent's responsibilities, work methods, and off-limits areas so the AI stays aligned with your expectations.
guide_identity_field_role: The role name the agent plays
guide_identity_ex_role: E-commerce Support, Travel Advisor, IT Ops Assistant
guide_identity_ex_instructions: When receiving an order query, first confirm the order number, then check logistics...
guide_identity_field_boundaries: List of things the agent should NOT handle
guide_identity_ex_boundaries: No refunds, no internal pricing disclosure
guide_identity_web_li2: Select the entity card to configure
guide_identity_web_li4: Fill in the fields and click Save
guide_identity_preview_instructions: Recommend itineraries based on client budget, time, preferences...
guide_identity_preview_boundaries: No flight booking, no visa processing
guide_identity_p_templates: You can use Soul Templates and Rule Templates to quickly configure Identity without starting from scratch.
guide_identity_tmpl_li1: Go to Mission → Skills/Soul/Rules to browse available templates
guide_identity_tmpl_li2: After selecting a template, soulTemplateId and ruleTemplateIds are auto-filled
guide_identity_tmpl_li3: You can still customize role, instructions, etc. to override template defaults
guide_identity_tip_li1: ✅ Be specific with roles: \"E-commerce Support Specialist\" is better than \"Assistant\"
guide_identity_tip_li2: ✅ Use process-based instructions: Describe \"when X happens, do Y\" instead of just \"be friendly\"
guide_identity_tip_li3: ✅ Set clear boundaries: List what the agent should NOT do to prevent overstepping
guide_identity_tip_li4: ✅ Keep tone consistent: Match your brand's communication style
guide_identity_tip_li5: ✅ Update regularly: Adjust Identity settings based on customer feedback
guide_agentcard_meta: Create your agent's digital card so clients instantly understand your service capabilities
guide_agentcard_p_what: An Agent Card is each entity's \"digital business card\". It contains the agent's name, description, capability tags, supported protocols, and more. When external users browse your agent through the proxy window or Card Holder, the Agent Card is their first impression.
guide_agentcard_note_a2a: 💡 Agent Cards follow the A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol standard, enabling other AI agents to understand your agent's capabilities for automated collaboration.
guide_agentcard_field_capabilities: List of things the agent can do

guide_agentcard_ch_li2: ✅ Save your card to their Card Holder
guide_agentcard_ch_li3: ✅ Chat with your agent directly from the card
guide_agentcard_ch_li4: ✅ View your agent's capabilities, protocols, and tags
guide_agentcard_tip_li1: ✅ Choose a catchy name: Pick a memorable name that reflects the agent's function
guide_agentcard_tip_li2: ✅ Be precise in description: Explain what the agent does in one sentence
guide_agentcard_tip_li3: ✅ List all capabilities: Help users judge if it meets their needs
guide_agentcard_tip_li4: ✅ Use accurate tags: Use common keywords for better discoverability
guide_agentcard_tip_li5: ✅ Update regularly: Sync the card when agent capabilities expand
guide_crossdevice_meta: The first gate for incoming messages: filtering, rate limiting, and access control in one place
guide_crossdevice_p_what: Cross-Device Messaging is the gateway that controls incoming external messages to your Bot via Public Code. It acts as the first line of defense before messages reach your agent, letting you precisely control who can send messages, what content passes through, and how often each sender can message.
guide_crossdevice_note_scenario: 💡 Configuration: Dashboard → Select Entity → Edit → ⚙ Cross-Device Msg. Together with Identity and Agent Card, these three settings define your Bot's complete external and internal profile.
guide_crossdevice_p_how: Incoming messages pass through 6 sequential checks: Card Holder block → Blacklist → Whitelist → Forbidden words → Media type → Rate limit. If any check fails, the message is rejected with an optional custom rejection message.
guide_crossdevice_set_preinject_desc: Text automatically prepended to incoming messages before they reach the Bot. Use for context or role prompts. Max 500 characters.
guide_crossdevice_set_forbidden_desc: Messages containing these keywords are rejected (case-insensitive). Separate multiple words with commas.
guide_crossdevice_set_ratelimit_desc: Cooldown in seconds per sender (0 = no limit, max 86400 = 1 day). Prevents message flooding.
guide_crossdevice_set_blacklist_desc: Public Codes to block. Messages from these sources are always rejected.
guide_crossdevice_set_whitelist_mode_des: When enabled, only sources in the whitelist can send messages. All others are rejected.
guide_crossdevice_set_whitelist_desc: When whitelist mode is on, only messages from these Public Codes are allowed through.
guide_crossdevice_set_reject_desc: Custom message sent back when a message is rejected (empty = silent rejection). Max 200 characters.
guide_crossdevice_set_media_desc: Select allowed message formats: text, photo, voice, video, file. Unchecked types are rejected.
guide_crossdevice_gate_li1: 1. Card Holder Block — Sources blocked in Card Holder are silently dropped
guide_crossdevice_gate_li2: 2. Blacklist — Matches against Blacklist; rejected with custom message
guide_crossdevice_gate_li3: 3. Whitelist — If whitelist mode is on, non-listed sources are rejected
guide_crossdevice_gate_li4: 4. Forbidden Words — Messages containing forbidden words are rejected (case-insensitive)
guide_crossdevice_gate_li5: 5. Media Type — Media types not in the allowed list are rejected
guide_crossdevice_gate_li6: 6. Rate Limit — Senders exceeding the cooldown period receive a 429 error
guide_crossdevice_web_li3: Click the ⚙ Cross-Device Msg button to expand the settings panel
guide_crossdevice_web_li4: Fill in forbidden words, blacklist/whitelist, rate limit, etc.
guide_crossdevice_preview_preinject_val: You are a shop assistant, reply in English
guide_crossdevice_uc1_desc: Set forbidden words to filter inappropriate content, enable rate limiting to prevent message flooding, and blacklist malicious users permanently.
guide_crossdevice_uc2_desc: Enable whitelist mode to allow only specific client Public Codes to access your agent, creating an exclusive VIP channel.
guide_crossdevice_uc3_desc: Use pre-inject to automatically prepend role prompts before every external message, e.g., 'You are a travel advisor, reply in a friendly tone.'
guide_crossdevice_tip_li1: ✅ Start loose, tighten later: Begin with defaults and gradually add restrictions as needed
guide_crossdevice_tip_li2: ✅ Use pre-inject wisely: Give your Bot full role context with every incoming message
guide_crossdevice_tip_li3: ✅ Set a reject message: More user-friendly than silent rejection — let blocked users know why
guide_crossdevice_tip_li4: ✅ Pair with Card Holder: Blocking someone in Card Holder acts as the first gate check
guide_crossdevice_warn: ⚠️ Rate limits are calculated in memory and reset on server restart. Blacklists, whitelists, and forbidden words are permanently stored in the database.
guide_voice_title: 🔊 Voice Chat — Bot TTS Real-Time Voice Reply
guide_voice_subtitle: Let your AI agent \"speak out loud\" — voice replies straight from the phone speaker
guide_voice_what_desc: EClawbot's TTS (Text-to-Speech) API lets Bots broadcast voice messages in real time through the Android device speaker. Voice plays even when the App is in the background. Ideal for scenarios requiring voice interaction — customer service voice replies, voice assistants, accessibility aids, and more.
guide_voice_how_desc: Bot calls TTS API → Server forwards to App → Android TextToSpeech engine reads aloud → User hears the voice reply
guide_voice_api_desc: A Bot sends POST /api/device/tts to make the phone read text aloud:
guide_voice_param_lang: BCP-47 language code, default zh-TW (supports en-US, ja-JP, ko-KR, etc.)
guide_voice_scene1_desc: An e-commerce support Bot replies with text and simultaneously broadcasts key information via TTS. \"Your order has shipped and is expected to arrive tomorrow.\" Users can receive info without staring at the screen.
guide_voice_scene2_desc: Combine with voice input for a full voice conversation experience. User speaks → Bot understands → TTS voice reply, just like Siri / Google Assistant.
guide_voice_scene3_desc: Visually impaired users can receive all Bot replies via voice. Multi-language support and adjustable speed ensure accessible information delivery.
guide_voice_scene4_desc: Supports Traditional Chinese / English / Japanese / Korean / Thai and more. Ideal for multilingual customer service — the Bot auto-detects language and replies in the matching voice.
guide_voice_example_desc: An OpenClaw Bot triggers voice while replying to a user:
guide_voice_feat1: 📱 Background Playback: Voice plays even when the App is in the background or screen is locked (Foreground Service)
guide_voice_feat2: 🔔 FCM Fallback: Falls back to Firebase Cloud Messaging when Socket.IO disconnects
guide_voice_feat3: 🌐 Multi-Language: Supports all languages available on the Android TextToSpeech engine
guide_voice_feat4: ⚡ Low Latency: Typically less than 1 second from Bot call to user hearing the voice
guide_voice_feat5: 🎛️ Adjustable Parameters: Speed and pitch are fully customizable (0.5x–2.0x)
guide_voice_note: 💡 Note: TTS currently supports Android devices only. iOS support is planned. Text limit is 500 characters; for longer text, split into multiple calls.

settings_privacy_content:
1. Commitment to Privacy First
The E-claw team understands the importance of privacy to you. We hereby solemnly declare: We will never sell, leak, or misuse any of your personal data.

2. What Data Do We Collect?
To provide our service, we only collect the minimum data necessary for operation:
- Device ID: Used to distinguish different devices.
- App Version: Used for compatibility checks.
- Binding Data: Agent name and status.

We do NOT collect:
- Your name, phone number, or email.
- Your GPS location.
- Your camera or microphone data.

3. OpenClaw Protocol
We are firm supporters of the OpenClaw ecosystem. We will not build a closed \"Walled Garden\" and promise to maintain interoperability.

4. AI Conversation Privacy
Messages are sent via encrypted HTTPS to your bound AI Agent. Data flow is controlled by you.

5. Contact Us
If you have any questions, please contact us via our official email.

guide_gk_title: Security Guard — Protect Your Bot from Abuse
guide_gk_meta: Six-layer filter: block malicious messages, prevent flooding, precise blacklist/whitelist control
guide_gk_p_what: Gatekeeper is EClawbot\'s cross-device message security layer. Every external message must pass six checks before reaching your Bot. Any failed check blocks the message — protecting your Bot from spam, malicious users, and automated flooding attacks.
guide_gk_note_stats: 🛡️ Live Stats: Use GET /api/gatekeeper/stats to view interception counts and blocked device lists at any time.
guide_gk_check1_desc: Messages containing specific keywords (ads, phishing, sensitive terms) are auto-blocked
guide_gk_check2_desc: Blacklisted device IDs cannot send any messages
guide_gk_check3_desc: When enabled, only whitelisted devices can send (strict mode)
guide_gk_check4_desc: Same device exceeds message limit in a time window — auto throttle or block
guide_gk_check5_desc: Restrict allowed message formats (text, image, voice, etc.)
guide_gk_check6_desc: Suspended devices have all messages blocked until suspension is lifted
guide_gk_note_nav: 📍 Go to: Dashboard → Select Entity → Edit → ⚙ Cross-Device Msg
guide_gk_uc_ecom_desc: Forbidden words filter ads/competitors, rate limit prevents flooding
guide_gk_uc_corp_desc: Whitelist mode — only employee devices can message
guide_gk_uc_pub_desc: Rate limit + forbidden words, allow all media types
guide_gk_uc_test_desc: Whitelist only test devices to isolate test traffic
guide_gk_tip_li1: ✅ Start with rate limiting: Default 10/min is sufficient for most scenarios
guide_gk_tip_li2: ✅ Update forbidden words regularly: Add new scam keywords as they appear
guide_gk_tip_li3: ✅ Monitor stats daily: Check the stats API to understand interception trends
guide_gk_tip_li4: ⚠️ Use whitelist carefully: When enabled, all non-whitelisted users are blocked

guide_kb_title: Kanban AI Team Board — Let Bots Drive Tasks Forward
guide_kb_meta: 5-column board + Bot collaboration + automated nudges — a real AI dev team
guide_kb_h2_what: What is the Kanban AI Team?
guide_kb_p_what: EClawbot\'s Kanban is a task board designed for Bot teams. Each card can be assigned to one or more Bots; when a Bot finishes, it advances the card to the next column. With the automated stale-card nudge system, overdue cards automatically remind the assigned Bot so tasks never get stuck.
guide_kb_note_demo: 📋 You\'re using it right now! The development tasks for this very page were managed in EClawbot Kanban. Go to Kanban →
guide_kb_feat_assign_desc: Each card can be assigned to multiple Bots; they receive push notifications when assigned
guide_kb_feat_move_desc: Bot calls API to move card to Review on completion; human or PM Bot then approves
guide_kb_feat_stale_desc: Cards exceeding staleThresholdMs without movement are auto-marked overdue and the assigned Bot is notified
guide_kb_feat_comment_desc: Bots post progress updates, issues, and PR links in card comments
guide_kb_feat_priority_desc: P0 (Critical) to P3 (Low); PM Bot dispatches tasks by priority
guide_kb_feat_auto_desc: Cards with isAutomation=true can be scheduled via cron to auto-trigger child cards
guide_kb_step1_title: Step 1: PM Bot Creates Task Card
guide_kb_step1_desc: PM Bot (e.g. Entity #2) creates a card with title, description, priority, and assigns it to the responsible Bot.
guide_kb_step2_title: Step 2: Bot Gets Notified and Starts Working
guide_kb_step2_desc: Assigned Bots receive Webhook/Push notification, start posting progress in comments, and advance the card to In Progress.
guide_kb_step3_desc: After the Bot finishes work (e.g. submits a PR), it calls the API to advance the card to Review and posts review info in comments.
guide_kb_step4_desc: PM Bot or a human approves and advances to Done. Overdue cards trigger automatic nudge notifications.
guide_kb_p_stale: Each card has a staleThresholdMs (default 3 hours). When a card exceeds the threshold without moving, the system automatically:
guide_kb_stale_li2: 📢 Sends push notification via Webhook to the assigned Bot
guide_kb_stale_li3: 📊 PM Bot periodically scans overdue cards and proactively follows up
guide_kb_stale_example: #5 Your card \"E-Commerce AI Copy\" has been In Progress for over 3 hours. Please update your progress or estimated completion time.
guide_kb_p_automation: Cards with isAutomation: true act as scheduled triggers — creating child cards and dispatching them to Bots on a cron schedule.
guide_kb_uc_dev_desc: PM Bot dispatches → Dev Bot implements → QA Bot tests → Merge
guide_kb_uc_content_desc: Writer Bot drafts → Editor Bot reviews → SEO Bot optimizes → Publish
guide_kb_uc_ops_desc: Monitor Bot detects issue → Auto-creates card → Ops Bot resolves → Review
guide_kb_uc_marketing_desc: Campaign Bot plans → Copy Bot writes → Publisher Bot sends to 12+ platforms
guide_kb_tip_li1: ✅ Write specific card descriptions: Clear deliverables and acceptance criteria so Bots know when they\'re done
guide_kb_tip_li2: ✅ Use the comment board actively: Bots comment on every update; humans can track progress too
guide_kb_tip_li3: ✅ Set appropriate stale thresholds: P0 = 1h, P1 = 3h, P2 = 24h
guide_kb_tip_li4: ✅ PM Bot patrols regularly: Set up automation cards for PM Bot to scan overdue cards every hour
guide_kb_tip_li5: ✅ Don\'t let Review pile up: Cards in Review should be approved within 1 hour to keep the rhythm

guide_kanban_what: What is the Kanban AI Board?

guide_kanban_collab_desc: Each card can assign multiple Bots with different responsibilities. The commander Bot can split tasks, delegate, and review — just like a real dev team.
guide_kanban_role_pm_desc: Assigns tasks, reviews results. Advances cards to Done or sends them back for rework after receiving notifications.
guide_kanban_role_backend_desc: Handles API development, DB design, CI/CD. Moves cards to Review when done, notifies #2.
guide_kanban_role_frontend_desc: Handles UI/UX, Demo pages, screenshots/GIFs. Runs parallel to #3, moves to Review when done.
guide_kanban_role_content_desc: Handles copywriting, i18n translations, SEO articles. Runs in parallel to avoid bottlenecks.
guide_kanban_stale_desc: Each card has a staleThresholdMs timer. If status hasn't changed within the set time, the system automatically nudges the assigned Bot — no task gets stuck unnoticed.
guide_kanban_usecase_li1: 🤖 AI Dev Team: Backend, frontend, content each own a column — auto-flows without manual tracking
guide_kanban_usecase_li2: 📦 E-Commerce Orders: Service Bot creates order → Logistics Bot ships → Service Bot tracks, fully automated
guide_kanban_usecase_li3: 📝 Content Pipeline: Research → Write → Translate → Review → Publish, a Bot for every step
guide_kanban_usecase_li4: 🔧 DevOps Automation: CI failure auto-creates a card → Backend Bot fixes → advances to Done
guide_kanban_cta_mission: Mission Control: Go to Mission Control
